definition: AI data center demand of 150-300+ TWh by 2028-2030 compounds the grid bottleneck by competing with renewable deployment for the same interconnection capacity, transformers, and copper.
evidence: PJM capacity prices reflecting real scarcity
Nuclear restarts (TMI, Palisades) demonstrating willingness to pay for firm baseload
Hyperscalers competing for same infrastructure as renewable buildout
Prior tech energy demand forecasts systematically overestimated (2007-2008: predicted 12% of US electricity by 2020, actual ~2-3%)
Current AI workloads near physical PUE limits (~1.1-1.2) limiting efficiency offset
open_questions: Will AI compute scaling or efficiency gains dominate the 150-300+ TWh range?
How much does hyperscaler infrastructure contracting crowd out renewable deployment?
Is the macro CPI impact (0.06-0.24pp) material or second-order?
